food_name: Beef Slice Rice with Tom Yum Broth

thai: ข้าวเนื้อต้มยำ

food_subgroup: Rice dishes with meat and broth

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Thai beef slice rice with Tom Yum broth specifically. FOODON_00002809 (beef dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (rice-based dish with meat) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Thai Tom Yum beef rice.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific Thai rice dish with beef and Tom Yum broth.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising roughly 200 g steamed jasmine rice, 120 g beef slices, and 130 g Tom Yum broth with aromatics and vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Beef with rice dish, estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Beef, short loin, top loin steak, boneless (NDB 23620) and Cooked white rice (NDB 20444), combined with Tom Yum soup component estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Thai Tom Yum beef slice rice exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked white rice (~200 g, ~130 kcal/100 g), lean beef slices (~120 g, ~175 kcal/100 g), and Tom Yum broth (~130 g, ~20 kcal/100 g) in proportions consistent with a 450 g restaurant serving. Weighted average per 100 g calculated. Sodium elevated due to fish sauce and broth. FAO/INFOODS SEA data used to cross-check Thai soup component sodium and micronutrient estimates.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; white rice (jasmine-type, cooked) GI ~68–72; beef and broth components contribute negligible GI
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 68 assigned based on jasmine white rice GI of 68 (Atkinson et al. 2021, AJCN). The dish is predominantly rice by carbohydrate contribution; beef and Tom Yum broth contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrates. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (68 × 70.2) / 100 ≈ 47.8. Available carbohydrate per 450 g serving = 15.6 × 4.5 = 70.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce and Tom Yum broth
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 380 mg sodium per 100 g (≈1,710 mg per 450 g serving), this dish provides over 70% of the WHO-recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal; individuals with hypertension should request reduced fish sauce and broth, or limit portion size significantly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from jasmine white rice
type2_diabetes.guidance: The dish carries a high glycemic load of approximately 47.8 per serving due to the large portion of jasmine white rice (GI ≈ 68); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider substituting brown rice or reducing the rice portion to manage postprandial blood glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The dish contains approximately 1.1 g saturated fat per 100 g and high sodium from the broth; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request lower-sodium preparation, and pair with vegetables to improve the overall dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Slice Rice with Tom Yum Broth (Thai: ข้าวเนื้อต้มยำ, romanized: Khao Nuea Tom Yum) is a Thai rice dish consisting of tender, thinly sliced beef served over steamed jasmine rice and accompanied by a spicy, sour Tom Yum broth. The broth is prepared by simmering lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime leaves, fresh chilli, fish sauce, and lime juice, producing a characteristically bold, aromatic, and tangy flavour profile. The beef is typically briefly cooked in the broth to retain tenderness. The dish is served as a complete one-plate meal and is popular in Thai restaurants in Singapore, including at Saap Saap Thai. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate source of protein (~9.8 g/100 g) from the beef, with carbohydrates predominantly from jasmine white rice (~15.6 g/100 g). Sodium content is elevated (~380 mg/100 g) due to fish sauce and broth, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is approximately 68 (medium), driven by jasmine rice, resulting in a high glycemic load per serving (~47.8 GL units), which is clinically relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is dairy-free and gluten-free in its traditional preparation but contains fish allergens from fish sauce.
common_user_queries: What is T2 Beef Slice Rice?

jasmine rice: khao suay (Thai: ข้าวสวย)
fish sauce: nam pla (Thai: น้ำปลา)
kaffir lime leaves: bai makrut (Thai: ใบมะกรูด)

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Thai Tom Yum beef slice rice was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition tables. Nutrient values were estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked white rice and lean beef with FAO/INFOODS SEA cross-checks for the Tom Yum broth component. GI is assigned by analogy to jasmine white rice from Atkinson et al. 2021 (AJCN), a peer-reviewed approved source. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae) is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used for this estimated composite dish. Folate (folate_mcg_dfe) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for the rice and beef components at the level of precision required. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food type. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this composite dish. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA SR Legacy data used for this estimation. Trans fat (trans_fat_g) value of 0.1 g/100 g reflects a small naturally occurring amount from ruminant beef (conjugated linoleic acid and vaccenic acid), consistent with USDA SR Legacy beef data. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed for the specific restaurant preparation without certification verification. The food_name has been normalised from the menu label 'T2 Beef Slice Rice' to the canonical English descriptor 'Beef Slice Rice with Tom Yum Broth'; the original menu name is preserved in aliases as 'T2 Beef Slice Rice'. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical Thai one-plate rice dish portions in Singapore restaurant context.

improvement_actions_taken: Normalised food_name from menu label 'T2 Beef Slice Rice' to canonical English 'Beef Slice Rice with Tom Yum Broth'; original menu name preserved in aliases.

Assigned GI of 68 by analogy to jasmine white rice from Atkinson et al. 2021 AJCN peer-reviewed source; GL per serving calculated and documented.
